# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

# pull base image
FROM alpine:latest

# install tools needed 
RUN apk add --no-cache \
    bash \
    nano

# set workdir to root in container
WORKDIR /app

# Add application files (you might need COPY command if your app is not based on alpine)
ADD . /app

# create directory for logs and cache
RUN mkdir -p /var/logs \
    && chown -R ${USER_UID:-1000}:${GROUP_GID:-1000} /var/logs 
    
RUN chmod -R 755 /var/logs

# set working directory to the project root (you might need this if your application needs it)
WORKDIR /app

# Open port for services that may be used by your app, e.g., 
EXPOSE 8080

# Run a command when container starts 
CMD ["bash"]
